manual QA/bug-hunting doesn't scale to agent-built apps; need automated, test-script-free bug detection that an agent (or CI) can act on
coding-agent sessions restart from scratch each time, forcing agents to repeatedly re-explore the same repo and re-derive context already learned in prior sessions
Running autonomous LLM agents with real access to accounts/systems is unsafe without provable isolation and human-in-the-loop approval for privilege changes.

AI coding agents run with full user credentials/shell access and can silently install backdoors or exfiltrate credentials; there's no visibility into what an agent actually did versus what it was asked to do.
AI agents need programmatic, natural-language-driven control of a live streaming/recording tool for hands-free production workflows.
embedding Claude Code/Agent SDK into other systems needs an HTTP-level session-management layer instead of raw CLI/SDK calls
Evaluating generalist robot policies across many robot/object/scene combinations requires an explosion of duplicated task configs; make environment variation compositional instead.
wants one conversational interface instead of separately operating Gmail, Calendar, and note-taking tools.
LLM coding agents lack precise semantic code navigation (go-to-definition, references, safe rename) that IDEs provide via LSP.
coding agents across sessions have no memory of prior architectural decisions and 'improve' code back into a state that violates decisions made in earlier sessions
let coding/chat agents read and write Feishu workspace data (docs, tasks, chat) without manual API wiring
Naive round-robin routing across inference backends wastes KV-cache locality and ignores load, hurting p95 latency and cost at scale.
coding agents/tools burn huge repeated/redundant LLM spend; teams want a self-hosted, air-gappable layer to deflect that traffic before it leaves infra
Hand-writing static-analysis checkers for the Linux kernel is slow and requires deep LLVM expertise; LLMs can learn checker patterns from real bug-fixing commits instead.

LLM-synthesized coding instruction data (e.g. Self-Instruct/Evol-Instruct) is biased toward common patterns the seed LLM already knows; grounding synthesis in real OSS snippets yields more diverse, realistic training data.
